food_name: Teh-C (Singapore tea with evaporated milk and sugar, hot — standard kopitiam preparation)
region_of_origin: Singapore — the tea equivalent of Kopi-C; strong Ceylon black tea served with Carnation evaporated milk and separately added white sugar at a traditional kopitiam
food_subgroup: Strong Ceylon black tea with evaporated milk (unsweetened, ~8% fat) and white sugar added separately

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic tea beverage entry (FOODON_03306128). No specific entry exists for Teh-C (Singapore kopitiam Ceylon black tea with evaporated milk and sugar).
ontology.foodex2_description: Tea, hot beverage — used as closest parent for Teh-C (Singapore kopitiam Ceylon black tea with evaporated milk and sugar)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 has no specific code for Teh-C. A0KZJ (tea) is used as the closest defensible parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Teh-C or Singapore kopitiam tea preparations.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is 1 cup (220 ml / 220 g), as served at a Singapore kopitiam.

nutrient_source.title: Teh-C (tea with evaporated milk and sugar) — Health Promotion Board Singapore, Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Teh-C (tea with evaporated milk and sugar, hot). HPB Foodo is the primary approved source for Singapore kopitiam beverages. Values are reported per 100 g of the prepared beverage as served. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and water content are not reported by HPB Foodo for this entry; these fields are set to -1 to indicate not reported by source.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index Database — milk tea and tea-with-milk analogues; estimated GI 65–72 for Ceylon black tea with evaporated milk and sugar
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Teh-C in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 66 is estimated from the closest analogous entries: tea with milk and sugar (GI ~65–72) as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database. Evaporated milk fat moderates the glycemic response relative to plain sugared tea. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (66 × 11.0) / 100 ≈ 7.2, where carbohydrate per serving = 5.0 g/100 g × 220 g / 100 = 11.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 20 mg per 100 g; caffeine lower than kopitiam coffee
hypertension.guidance: Teh-C contains very low sodium (20 mg/100 g; ~44 mg per 220 g serving) and provides lower caffeine (~50–75 mg per cup) than Robusta-based kopitiam coffee (~100–150 mg), making it a suitable beverage choice for hypertensive patients who are caffeine-sensitive.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI 66 (medium), glycemic load 7.2 per serving, sugar ~9.5 g per cup
type2_diabetes.guidance: Teh-C has a moderate GI (66) and a low glycemic load per serving (7.2), representing a meaningfully better choice than standard Teh (condensed milk); for patients with Type 2 Diabetes, requesting Teh-C Siu Dai (less sugar) further reduces the glycemic load and is the preferred kopitiam tea option.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.1 g per 100 g (~2.4 g per serving); no trans fat
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 220 g serving of Teh-C contributes approximately 2.4 g of saturated fat and 0 g of trans fat; while the saturated fat contribution is modest, patients with cardiovascular disease who consume multiple kopitiam beverages daily should consider switching to Teh-O (black tea without milk) to further reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh-C is a classic Singapore kopitiam hot tea beverage made from strong-brewed Ceylon black tea concentrate combined with unsweetened evaporated milk (commonly Carnation brand, ~8% fat) and separately added white sugar. It occupies the middle tier of the kopitiam tea matrix: richer and creamier than Teh-O (black tea with sugar only) but less sweet than standard Teh (which uses sweetened condensed milk). The 'C' in Teh-C refers to Carnation evaporated milk, distinguishing it from the condensed-milk version. Per 100 g, Teh-C provides approximately 36 kcal, 0.9 g protein, 1.7 g fat (1.1 g saturated), and 5.0 g carbohydrate (4.3 g sugar), with a sodium content of only 20 mg. A standard 220 ml cup delivers roughly 79 kcal, 11 g carbohydrate, and 9.5 g sugar — approximately 50% less sugar than standard Teh. The glycemic index is estimated at 66 (medium) with a glycemic load of 7.2 per serving, making it a clinically preferable kopitiam beverage for patients managing Type 2 Diabetes or weight. For clinicians counselling patients on beverage upgrades, the kopitiam tea ladder from highest to lowest sugar impact is: Teh → Teh Siu Dai → Teh-C → Teh-C Siu Dai → Teh-O Siu Dai → Teh-O Kosong.
common_user_queries: teh c vs kopi c calories

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (exact match for Teh-C, tea with evaporated milk and sugar). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using the closest tea-with-milk-and-sugar analogues, clearly documented as an estimate.
notes: Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and water content are not reported by HPB Foodo for this entry and are set to -1 (not reported by source). Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g (confirmed negligible by source). GI is an analogous estimate; no direct Teh-C entry exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Hindi local name and alias added to satisfy Singaporean dish multilingual requirements.
